payload.title: Operator-surface label incident follow-up observations
payload.summary: After the duplicate-label/crossbinding incident was contained, several adjacent design observations became clear. These are not all separate CAPAs, but they should inform Narada operator-surface hardening.

observations.title: Foreground-based mutation is actively misleading
observations.detail: The command name Set-FocusedWindowIdentityBinding invited use of ambient focus even though operator-surface identity repair requires explicit captured target authority. Naming should reflect the admitted authority source.

observations.title: Runtime bindings need a doctor command
observations.detail: Operators/architects should not manually inspect JSON for duplicate HWNDs, duplicate identities, stale HWNDs, dead windows, or crossbinding. A compact health/doctor command should report and optionally repair with evidence.

observations.title: OSM should preflight binding uniqueness
observations.detail: OSM sent next to Bob but resolved Bob to Kevin while runtime bindings were polluted. OSM must refuse ambiguous or incoherent binding posture before focus/sendkeys delivery.

observations.title: Overlay should self-deduplicate
observations.detail: Even if the runtime binding file is bad, the overlay must not draw two labels on one HWND. It should render no label or one deterministic label plus diagnostics.

observations.title: Repair operations need transaction evidence
observations.detail: Manual runtime state edits repaired the incident but did not create a durable PC Site repair evidence artifact. Binding repair should write before/after evidence and postcondition checks.

observations.title: Window title is weak identity evidence
observations.detail: Windows Terminal titles mutate with spinner/context/session state. Binding confidence should rely on HWND, PID, class, process, and stronger WT/session/profile evidence rather than title text.

observations.title: Label projection and runtime binding are distinct authority layers
observations.detail: Rebuilding label projection does not repair runtime HWND bindings. Current UX makes these look like one operation, hiding the split and encouraging incomplete repairs.

requested_direction: Treat these as design inputs for operator-surface binding, OSM delivery, overlay rendering, and PC Site runtime evidence.
Consider adding an operator-surface health command that reports binding uniqueness, overlay label count, stale HWNDs, and OSM readiness.
Clarify command names and docs so explicit captured HWND is the only admitted mutation target for identity binding.
